completed_tasks.name: Revert Schedule coupling in runDailyDigest, move cron to once-daily 09:00 ET
Switch digest bucket matching from rule.name to rule.systemType
Grey out DigestSettingsForm (settings page) — PR #22 missed this duplicated form
Update urgent_uncertain_delete_risk memory to reflect narrowed scope

remaining_tasks.name: Confirm 09:00 ET arrival + non-zero Notifications section in next morning's digest
Merge PR #23 (DigestSettingsForm disable) on next code-change deploy — user opted not to deploy alone for such a minor fix

human_actions_pending.context: Verify hotfix bug #1 and #2 in production after auto-deploy of 57fa0fe11

human_actions_pending.action: Merge PR #23 next time we touch the code
human_actions_pending.context: Bundle with next deploy to avoid spinning up a build for a cosmetic UI fix

decisions.decision: Drop fork-only Schedule advance reimplementation in runDailyDigest rather than re-debug
decisions.rationale: Minimize upstream divergence; single-user fork doesn't need per-account scheduling

decisions.decision: systemType-first bucket matching with name fallback for Urgent/Uncertain
decisions.rationale: Survive rule renames without adding new SystemType enum values (which would mean Prisma migration + further divergence)

decisions.decision: Grey out UI form with tooltip rather than hide it
decisions.rationale: Keeps upstream component intact; makes the server-side override visible and explainable

decisions.decision: Defer PR #23 deploy until bundled with next code change
decisions.rationale: Cosmetic-only fix; not worth the build cycle on its own

next_action: At ~09:05 ET on 2026-05-12 confirm digest arrival time and Notifications section content; if either fails, check inbox-zero-cron and inbox-zero-app docker logs. Separately: when next deploying anything, ensure PR #23 is included in the merge.
context_notes: This session: investigated why the 'greyed out digest time' from PR #22 still shows editable in production. Root cause: two near-duplicate digest schedule forms exist. PR #22 disabled DigestScheduleForm.tsx (used only by the onboarding StepDigest pages); the actual settings page at /assistant/settings → Digest renders DigestSettingsForm.tsx, which has its own copy of frequency Select + day-of-week Select + TimePicker and was never touched. Opened PR #23 mirroring PR #22's disabling pattern on the settings form. User opted to merge it next time we touch the code rather than deploy alone. Worth noting for future work: the two forms duplicate ~150 lines of schedule UI — extract a shared component next time someone touches this area.
